title: Ballots, Tallies, and Audits
summary: Append ballots to an immutable log; aggregate counts in stream processors or materialized views.
keyTakeaways: Ballot ingest API validates voter eligibility once, writes immutable record.
Hash chain or signed events support third-party audit (high-stakes systems).
Tally service aggregates by choice_id; real-time preview uses stream processor.
Results page reads materialized count — never COUNT(*) on raw ballots live.
Idempotency-Key per voter session prevents double submit on retry.
Geo-distributed read replicas for results; single-region write primary for consistency.

body.source:
flowchart TB Voter["Voter app"] Log[("Ballot Log
append-only")] Results[("Results Cache
Redis")] Agg["Stream Aggregator"] subgraph writePath ["Write path — cast ballot (append-only)"] direction LR API["Ballot API
idempotent"] Voter -->|"① POST + Idempotency-Key"| API API -->|"② INSERT ballot"| Log API -->|"③ ballot.cast event"| Agg end subgraph readPath ["Read path — live totals (never COUNT on log)"] direction LR Agg -->|"④ INCR choice_count"| Results Voter -->|"⑤ GET /results"| Results end
body.caption: Votes are append-only writes; live totals are pre-aggregated in Redis — never COUNT(*) on the ballot table at read time.

items: ① POST + Idempotency-Key — Voter submits once; retries return the same ballot ID, not a duplicate vote.
② INSERT ballot — Append-only row in ballot log; ballots are never UPDATEd in place.
③ ballot.cast event — Stream aggregator notified asynchronously to update live totals.
④ INCR choice_count — Aggregator increments precomputed counts in Redis on each ballot event.
⑤ GET /results — Results page reads materialized totals from cache — O(1) per choice, not a table scan.

body.source:
sequenceDiagram
    participant V as Voter
    participant API as Ballot API
    participant DB as Ballot Log
    participant Agg as Aggregator
    participant C as Results Cache
    V->>API: POST /ballots + Idempotency-Key
    API->>DB: INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
    DB-->>API: ballotId
    API->>Agg: ballot.cast event
    API-->>V: 201 accepted
    Note over Agg,C: async tally
    Agg->>C: INCR choice_count
    V->>C: GET /results
    C-->>V: live totals
body.caption: Idempotent vote submission and async tally update to results cache.
Idempotent ballot submission

body.title: Slack at scale
body.text: Workspace polls are low-stakes but still use one-vote-per-user keys stored in OLTP with unique indexes — the same pattern at national scale with harder identity proofing.

body.title: Why PostgreSQL (append-only ballot log)?
body.text: UNIQUE (election_id, voter_id) enforces one vote per person at the database layer. Append-only inserts preserve audit trail; tallies are derived, never UPDATE-in-place on ballot rows.

body.title: Why Idempotency-Key header?
body.text: Network retries must not create duplicate ballots — same key returns the same result. Simpler than distributed locks for voter sessions.

body.title: Why Kafka / stream processor?
body.text: Aggregates millions of ballots into live totals without scanning the full table on every results page refresh. Handles write spikes when polls close.

body.title: Why Redis / materialized results cache?
body.text: Results pages are read-heavy during election night — precomputed counts serve sub-ms reads. Eventual consistency of +1–2 seconds is usually acceptable for display.

body.title: Why API gateway + rate limiting (Cloudflare / WAF)?
body.text: Bot protection and per-IP throttling at the edge before ballots hit your origin. Critical when viral links drive traffic spikes.

body.title: Why Not updating vote counts in the ballot row?
body.text: In-place UPDATE loses auditability and races under concurrency. Event sourcing pattern: insert ballot, derive count.
